summary_one_line: Two or more uniform 4-byte glyph records all categorized VPre (left matra), so >=2 distinct POS_PRE_M indices exist post-sort.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A run of repeated near-identical 4-byte little-endian glyph records (e.g. 00 10 00 00 / 84 10 00 00) that the Myanmar shaper categorizes as multiple M_Cat(VPre) left-matras, yielding >=2 glyphs assigned POS_PRE_M so first_left_matra < last_left_matra.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: naive winner seeds are clean periodic 4-byte records (00 10 00 00 then 84 10 00 00 repeated); the per-role overlay shows W taking lines 234-237 (VPre -> POS_PRE_M) and 280-285 multiple times, then line 288 true (W T=5 F=0).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: cmplog loser seeds are heterogeneous noise (f9 runs, token fragments like hasc/csah, GMOSH-like bytes at 0x0c-0x0f) that do not produce a multi-VPre run; L never reaches POS_PRE_M (line 280 L T=0 F=20) and line 288 is false-only (L T=0 F=17).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: The winner is naive, which has only the havoc+token mutator stack and no input-to-state substitution. Plain havoc byte mutation (ByteNeg/BitFlip/BytesExpand/QwordAdd on an already-periodic seed) preserves and extends the repeating 4-byte record run, accumulating multiple VPre matras. cmplog's extra comparison-solving mutator substitutes CMP-derived operands into the input, derailing it toward heterogeneous CMP-matching content that breaks the uniform multi-matra run; so the comparison-solving technique is actively counterproductive here and havoc-only naive wins.

evidence_trail.claim: The decisive pair is naive > cmplog with the I2S technique delta, naive the winner and cmplog the loser.
The winning side is the true branch of first_left_matra < last_left_matra, which requires two distinct left-matra indices.
naive winner seeds are clean periodic 4-byte records of the form 00 10 00 00 then repeated 84 10 00 00.
The winner-only overlay reaches the VPre left-matra assignment of POS_PRE_M.
The winner takes the blocker true side while the loser only takes the false side.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 288  T=5 F=0  T=0 F=17  if (first_left_matra < last_left_matra)  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: The loser never sets any glyph to POS_PRE_M, so it cannot populate first/last left-matra.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 280  T=17 F=6  T=0 F=20  if (info[i].myanmar_position() == POS_PRE_M)

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog loser seeds are heterogeneous and carry token-like fragments unlike the periodic winner records.
The winner is naive, whose mutator set is havoc + token with no I2S.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog adds I2SRandReplace which substitutes CMP-derived operands into the input at matching offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: The winner is naive (no I2S mutator), so lineage I2S-floor verification does not apply; the mechanism enabling the winner is plain havoc/token byte mutation preserving the periodic 4-byte glyph-record run, and I2S is the LOSER's distinguishing technique here (counterproductive), so no I2SRandReplace claim is made.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness where cmplog (with I2S) resolves first_left_matra < last_left_matra at least as fast as naive on inputs of repeating uniform VPre-categorized glyph records, showing I2S does not derail the multi-matra run.

weakest_evidence_point: The exact byte-to-myanmar_category mapping is inferred from seed structure and overlay hits, not directly observed; the periodic record could be hitting VPre via a different code path than assumed.
